panels.title: Proxy: request rate by endpoint (downstream client → proxy)
Proxy: upstream calls per request (P50 / P95)
Proxy: cache hit ratio (L1 memory, 5 min)
Proxy: downstream P50 / P95 / P99 by endpoint
Proxy: upstream (VL) backend P95 by endpoint
Proxy: cache hit ratio by tier (L0 hot-index / L1 mem / L2 disk / L3 peer)
Proxy: cache size by tier — bytes resident
Proxy: cache objects by tier (L0 = hot-index population)
Proxy — peer cache communication (3-node ring: proxy-0/1/2)
Peer ring topology — discovered peers vs cluster members vs L0 hot keys
Per-instance cache hit ratio by tier (proxy-0 / proxy-1 / proxy-2 × L0/L1/L2/L3)
Peer cache: cross-peer fetches (hits = served from a peer; misses = went to backend)
Peer write-through: cache pushes from non-owner peers to owner peer
Peer read-ahead: hot-key prefetch from peers (prewarms local cache from hot peer keys)
Peer read-ahead bytes prefetched (network volume warming the local L1)
Peer hot-index requests + errors (the L0-driven peer-to-peer key advertisement layer)
Peer cache errors by reason (transport / timeout / decode / token / etc.)
Proxy — heap deep-dive (why is heap growing?)
Proxy: heap alloc (live objects)  vs  heap sys (OS reservation)  vs  RSS
Proxy: allocation rate (mallocs/s, frees/s) — should be roughly balanced post-GC
Proxy: GC pressure (cycles/s, pause time/s, next-GC target)
VL: HTTP request duration (P99 summary) by path
Proxy: stack + other runtime memory (typically small)
VL: storage rows / data on disk
Loki: request P95 latency by route
Loki: embedded cache — hit / miss / eviction

title: Bench: Drilldown vs Loki — resources, traffic, cache, latency
